summary: Matched a batch of item/Pokemon-related functions across 12 item C files, replacing /// # stubs with 100% matching C and tightening several item attribute/prototype declarations. The PR body says 20 item functions were decompiled and verified with a clean ninja build plus fuzzy_match_percent 100.0; the decomp.dev bot reported +2276 matched code bytes, +16 matched data bytes, and 21 new matches including one .sdata2 match.

key_files.role: Expanded item attribute layouts for Kyasarin and Houou, and corrected Mewtwo Shadowball item var x4C from f32 to s32.
Matched Kyasarin motion anim/coll callbacks using velocity and boundary attributes.
Matched Houou physics and map-speed movement helpers; added mp/mplib.h include.
Matched Mewtwo Shadowball physics/reflection logic using sinf/cosf and angle wrapping; added math.h.
Matched Matadogas spawned callbacks with efSync_Spawn effect IDs; added ef/efsync.h.
Matched Game & Watch Chef animation timer/flicker callback.
Matched Hitodeman physics transition and hitlag callbacks.
Replaced UNK_RET/UNK_PARAMS for it_802CB798 with void it_802CB798(Item_GObj*).
Replaced UNK_RET/UNK_PARAMS for it_802C4D10 with void it_802C4D10(Item_GObj*).
Replaced UNK_RET/UNK_PARAMS for it_802E3400 with void it_802E3400(Item_GObj*).
Replaced UNK_RET/UNK_PARAMS for it_802DF9F8 and it_802DFF14 with typed void prototypes.

what_changed: Replaced item function stubs with matched implementations in itdosei, itgamewatchchef, ithitodeman, ithouou, itkyasarin, itmatadogas, itmewtwoshadowball, itoldkuri, itoldottosea, ittincle, itwhitebea, and itzgshell.

Matched functions listed in the PR body include it_2725_Logic101_Reflected, it_2725_Logic32_Spawned, it_2725_Logic33_Spawned, it_3F14_Logic7_EnteredAir, fn_80281734, it_802D2F70, itHouou_UnkMotion3_Phys, it_802DF9F8, it_802E3400, it_802EC3F4, itGamewatchchef_UnkMotion1_Anim, itHitodeman_UnkMotion2_Phys, several itKyasarin_UnkMotion callbacks, itMewtwoshadowball_UnkMotion8_Phys, itOldkuri_UnkMotion0_Phys, itOldkuri_UnkMotion1_Coll, and itOldottosea_UnkMotion7_Anim.
Adjusted itKyasarinAttributes to expose x28 and x2C bounds after explicit padding, enabling Kyasarin collision checks against position x.
Adjusted itHououAttr padding to expose x10, used as Houou z velocity while its timer is active.
Corrected itMewtwoShadowball_ItemVars x4C from f32 to s32, matching its use as an integer countdown/flag in physics.
Concretized several item helper prototypes from UNK_RET/UNK_PARAMS to typed Item_GObj signatures in headers.
Added needed includes for newly referenced APIs: ef/efsync.h, mp/mplib.h, and math.h.
smart_moves: Fixed shared struct layouts before using newly discovered fields, instead of hiding offset access in local casts.
Changed Mewtwo Shadowball x4C to s32 because the matched code tests it as an integer greater-than-zero condition.
Used typed helper prototypes in headers once call signatures were known, improving future callsite matching and reducing unknown macros.
Preserved item state-machine conventions: GET_ITEM/gobj->user_data access, Item_80268E5C motion changes, ITEM_ANIM_UPDATE, and bool Anim/Coll return patterns.
Matched projectile reflection with explicit angle addition and while-loop normalization rather than a higher-level modulo helper.
Used effect/map/math subsystem APIs directly where behavior required them: efSync_Spawn for Matadogas effects, mpLib/mpGetSpeed for Houou movement, and sinf/cosf for Mewtwo Shadowball velocity.
Kept stack/layout-sensitive artifacts such as PAD_STACK and UNUSED locals where the diff shows they were needed for 100% matches.
decomp_lessons: Item attribute structs should be expanded with explicit offset comments and padding as soon as matched functions prove new fields; this PR exposed Kyasarin x28/x2C and Houou x10.
Do not assume f32 for unknown item vars just because nearby fields are floats; Mewtwo Shadowball x4C needed to be s32 for matching integer control flow.
Projectile angle wrapping in matched C may need simple while loops with M_PI/M_TAU, not fmodf or other compact math helpers.
Many item animation callbacks follow a reusable pattern: check it_80272C6C, set velocities/nudge/state fields, switch motion with Item_80268E5C, and return false until completion.
Spawn callbacks may require a local f32 scale pointer and exact effect IDs, plus stack padding, to match efSync_Spawn call sequences.
Header prototype cleanup is part of matching work: removing UNK_RET/UNK_PARAMS helps stabilize future decompilation and makes call semantics visible.
When helper calls may clobber assumptions or affect scheduling, re-reading gobj->user_data and matching local variable order can be necessary.
naming_conventions: Item source files remain lowercase item names under src/melee/it/items, such as itkyasarin.c, ithouou.c, and itmewtwoshadowball.c.
Known item callbacks use item-prefixed names like itDosei, itGamewatchchef, itHitodeman, itHouou, itKyasarin, itMewtwoshadowball, itOldkuri, itOldottosea, itTincle, itZrshell, and itZGShell.
Unidentified functions retain address-based names such as it_802D2F70, it_802DF9F8, it_802E3400, it_802EC3F4, and fn_80281734.
Logic callback names follow patterns such as it_2725_LogicNN_Spawned and it_2725_LogicNN_Reflected.
Motion callbacks follow ItemName_UnkMotionN_Anim, ItemName_UnkMotionN_Phys, and ItemName_UnkMotionN_Coll; Anim/Coll are commonly bool and Phys is commonly void.
Unknown struct fields stay offset-based, with names like x4, x10, x28, x2C, x4C, and explicit xC_pad/x14_pad padding until semantics are understood.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: PAD_STACK(4) was used in Matadogas spawned callbacks, and PAD_STACK(8) appears in Oldkuri/Oldottosea matched functions to preserve stack layout.
An UNUSED u32 _pad local in it_2725_Logic33_Spawned helped reproduce the expected local layout.
The Matadogas spawn variants differ in local initialization order: one initializes scale immediately, while the other declares scale, pads stack, then assigns 1.0f.
The Mewtwo Shadowball reflection uses while loops against 0.0f and M_TAU after adding M_PI, matching low-level branch structure.
The Mewtwo Shadowball physics computes velocity from polar components with cosf and sinf only when x4C > 0, then always calls it_802C4D10.
Tincle reset code writes z, y, then x components for both velocity and nudge, a store order worth preserving in similar vector-zeroing matches.
itDosei_EnteredAir uses a scoped HSD_JObj local and sets x5D0_animFrameSpeed plus lb_8000BA0C before clearing owner, showing local scope/order can matter.
itzgshell keeps an explicit cast in Item_80268E5C((HSD_GObj*) gobj, 0xA, ITEM_ANIM_UPDATE), evidence that exact argument typing can affect matching.
review_feedback: No human review comments or formal review records were present in the slice.
The decomp.dev bot reported matched code 55.94% (+0.06%, +2276 bytes) and matched data 37.51% (+0.00%, +16 bytes).

The bot listed 21 new matches, including the item functions plus an itgamewatchchef .sdata2 match; it also noted one itmewtwoshadowball .sdata2 improvement and a small itoldkuri .sdata2 regression in an unmatched item.
The PR body reported a clean ninja build and fuzzy_match_percent 100.0 for all functions.

The PR body mentions itTincle_UnkMotion7_Coll, but the provided diff and bot match table show it_802EC3F4 for ittincle; evidence for an actual itTincle_UnkMotion7_Coll implementation in this slice is weak.
follow_up_queries: Search remaining src/melee/it/items /// # stubs for the same Item_80268E5C plus it_80272C6C motion-transition pattern.
Audit item attribute structs adjacent to itKyasarinAttributes, itHououAttr, and itMewtwoShadowball_ItemVars for more fields whose types/padding are now inferable.
Look for UNK_RET/UNK_PARAMS prototypes in item headers that can be concretized from known Item_GObj callsites.
Compare future ittincle work against this PR because the PR body names itTincle_UnkMotion7_Coll but the diff/bot evidence only confirms it_802EC3F4.
Check .sdata2 effects around math constants and float literals in itmewtwoshadowball, itgamewatchchef, and itoldkuri when matching nearby functions.
